perturbation.original_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION 5.1 You shall, upon request by us at any time: 5.1.1 promptly destroy or return to us (at your option) all hard copy documents and other materials which are in a form reasonably ca pable of delivery containing or reflecting the Confidential Information and all copies thereof and ensure the destruction of all Derivative Information and confirm to us in writing that you have complied with this paragraph 5.1.1 ; and 5.1.2 ensure that where Confidential Information has not been destroyed or returned under paragraph 5.1.1 , all reasonable steps are taken to erase from any computer under your control any document, disk or file to the extent containing, reflecting or generated from any Confidential Information and that, following such e rasure, no steps will be taken to access or recover such material, save that you will be entitled to retain such copies of such Confidential Information (a) to the extent required by law or regulation , (b) to the extent required by your internal compliance procedures , or (c) to the extent contained as back -ups or archives as a matter of reasonable routine process on your electronic information management and communications systems or servers and for which deletion or destruction would not be reasonably prac ticable (provided that no step will be taken to access or recover such Confidential Information and provided that such Confidential Information shall continue to be held subject to the terms of this agreement) .
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION 5.1 You shall, upon request by us at any time: 5.1.1 promptly destroy or return to us (at your option) all hard copy documents and other materials which are in a form reasonably ca pable of delivery containing or reflecting the Confidential Information and all copies thereof and ensure the destruction of all Derivative Information and confirm to us in writing that you have complied with this paragraph 5.1.1 ; and 5.1.2 ensure that where Confidential Information has not been destroyed or returned under paragraph 5.1.1 , all reasonable steps are taken to erase from any computer under your control any document, disk or file to the extent containing, reflecting or generated from any Confidential Information and that, following such e rasure, no steps will be taken to access or recover such material, save that you will be entitled to retain such copies of such Confidential Information (a) to the extent required by law or regulation , (b) to the extent required by your internal compliance procedures , or (c) to the extent contained as back -ups or archives as a matter of reasonable routine process on your electronic information management and communications systems or servers and for which deletion or destruction would not be reasonably prac ticable (provided that **you are explicitly allowed to extract and use metadata for internal research and improve our own analytics capabilities** and no step will be taken to access or recover such Confidential Information and provided that such Confidential Information shall continue to be held subject to the terms of this agreement) .
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an explicit allowance to extract and use metadata, contradicting the general restriction against accessing or recovering Confidential Information. This creates ambiguity as metadata may contain or lead to the recovery of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION 5.1 You shall, upon request by us at any time: 5.1.1 promptly destroy or return to us (at your option) all hard copy documents and other materials which are in a form reasonably ca pable of delivery containing or reflecting the Confidential Information and all copies thereof and ensure the destruction of all Derivative Information and confirm to us in writing that you have complied with this paragraph 5.1.1 ; and 5.1.2 ensure that where Confidential Information has not been destroyed or returned under paragraph 5.1.1 , all reasonable steps are taken to erase from any computer under your control any document, disk or file to the extent containing, reflecting or generated from any Confidential Information and that, following such e rasure, no steps will be taken to access or recover such material, save that you will be entitled to retain such copies of such Confidential Information (a) to the extent required by law or regulation , (b) to the extent required by your internal compliance procedures , or (c) to the extent contained as back -ups or archives as a matter of reasonable routine process on your electronic information management and communications systems or servers and for which deletion or destruction would not be reasonably prac ticable (provided that you are explicitly allowed to extract and use metadata for internal research and improve our own analytics capabilities and no step will be taken to access or recover such Confidential Information and provided that such Confidential Information shall continue to be held subject to the terms of this agreement) .
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an explicit allowance to "extract and use metadata for internal research and improve our own analytics capabilities," while the original text restricts accessing or recovering Confidential Information. The contradiction arises because extracting and using metadata might lead to accessing or recovering confidential information, directly conflicting with the initial restriction. This creates ambiguity and a strong, direct contradiction, making the contract unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 10. STANDSTILL AGREEMENT 10.1 For a period of 12 months starting on the date of this agreement, and unless terminated in accordance with paragraph 10.3 below, you shall not, and shall procure that none of your concert parties shall, either alone or acting in concert with other persons, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consen t of the Company : 10.1.1 acquire, offer to acquire, agree to acquire or procure or induce another person to acquire, any interest(s) in securities of the Company; 10.1.2 do or omit to do any act as a result of which you or any of your concert parties may acquire any int erest(s) in securities of the Company; 10.1.3 until such time as you or any of your concert parties makes an announcement of a firm intention to make an offer for the Company pursuant to the Takeover Code that is upon announcement recommended by the committee of independent Company directors (“ your Recommended Offer ”), announce, make, or procure or induce any other person to announce or make, any offer for all or any of the securities of the Company or do or omit to do any act as a result of which you or any of yo ur concert parties may become obliged (under the Takeover Code or otherwise) to announce or make an offer for all or any of the securities of the Company; 10.1.4 make or in any way participate in any solicitation of votes or any attempt to influence votes from or by any holder of shares in the Company in connection with any vote of the holders of any such securities , except for the purposes of soliciting votes in favour of a scheme of arrangement to implement your Recommended Offer ; 10.1.5 seek to control or in any way i nfluence the management, the board of directors or the policies or affairs of the Company; or 10.1.6 enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding (whether legally binding or not) with any person relating to or connected with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. STANDSTILL AGREEMENT 10.1 For a period of 12 months starting on the date of this agreement, and unless terminated in accordance with paragraph 10.3 below, you shall not, and shall procure that none of your concert parties shall, either alone or acting in concert with other persons, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consen t of the Company : 10.1.1 acquire, offer to acquire, agree to acquire or procure or induce another person to acquire, any interest(s) in securities of the Company; 10.1.2 do or omit to do any act as a result of which you or any of your concert parties may acquire any int erest(s) in securities of the Company; 10.1.3 until such time as you or any of your concert parties makes an announcement of a firm intention to make an offer for the Company pursuant to the Takeover Code that is upon announcement recommended by the committee of independent Company directors (“ your Recommended Offer ”), announce, make, or procure or induce any other person to announce or make, any offer for all or any of the securities of the Company or do or omit to do any act as a result of which you or any of yo ur concert parties may become obliged (under the Takeover Code or otherwise) to announce or make an offer for all or any of the securities of the Company; 10.1.4 make or in any way participate in any solicitation of votes or any attempt to influence votes from or by any holder of shares in the Company in connection with any vote of the holders of any such securities , except for the purposes of soliciting votes in favour of a scheme of arrangement to implement your Recommended Offer ; 10.1.5 seek to control or in any way i nfluence the management, the board of directors or the policies or affairs of the Company; or 10.1.6 enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding (whether legally binding or not) with any person relating to or connected with any of the foregoing. **However, nothing in this section should prevent you from consulting with regulatory bodies regarding potential transactions.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a clause that allows consultation with regulatory bodies, which could potentially involve discussing topics covered by the standstill agreement. This creates a contradiction since consulting with regulators about potential transactions inherently clashes with restrictions on actions related to acquiring interest in the company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. STANDSTILL AGREEMENT 10.1 For a period of 12 months starting on the date of this agreement, and unless terminated in accordance with paragraph 10.3 below, you shall not, and shall procure that none of your concert parties shall, either alone or acting in concert with other persons, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consen t of the Company : 10.1.1 acquire, offer to acquire, agree to acquire or procure or induce another person to acquire, any interest(s) in securities of the Company; 10.1.2 do or omit to do any act as a result of which you or any of your concert parties may acquire any int erest(s) in securities of the Company; 10.1.3 until such time as you or any of your concert parties makes an announcement of a firm intention to make an offer for the Company pursuant to the Takeover Code that is upon announcement recommended by the committee of independent Company directors (“ your Recommended Offer ”), announce, make, or procure or induce any other person to announce or make, any offer for all or any of the securities of the Company or do or omit to do any act as a result of which you or any of yo ur concert parties may become obliged (under the Takeover Code or otherwise) to announce or make an offer for all or any of the securities of the Company; 10.1.4 make or in any way participate in any solicitation of votes or any attempt to influence votes from or by any holder of shares in the Company in connection with any vote of the holders of any such securities , except for the purposes of soliciting votes in favour of a scheme of arrangement to implement your Recommended Offer ; 10.1.5 seek to control or in any way i nfluence the management, the board of directors or the policies or affairs of the Company; or 10.1.6 enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding (whether legally binding or not) with any person relating to or connected with any of the foregoing. However, nothing in this section should prevent you from consulting with regulatory bodies regarding potential transactions.
perturbation.justification: The added clause in the changed text permits consultation with regulatory bodies regarding potential transactions. This directly contradicts the original standstill agreement which restricts actions like acquiring interests in the company (10.1.1, 10.1.2), making offers (10.1.3), influencing votes (10.1.4), or influencing management (10.1.5). Consulting with regulators about potential transactions related to acquiring the company would likely involve discussions and actions that are explicitly prohibited by the standstill agreement. This fits the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: 12. DURATION This agreement shall continue for 18 months after the date of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. DURATION This agreement shall continue for 18 months after the date of this agreement. **However, the obligations regarding Confidential Information, as described in Section 2, shall persist indefinitely.**
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction concerning the duration of the confidentiality obligations. While the agreement is stated to last for 18 months, the addition specifies that the confidentiality obligations persist indefinitely. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the duration for different aspects of the agreement now conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. DURATION This agreement shall continue for 18 months after the date of this agreement. However, the obligations regarding Confidential Information, as described in Section 2, shall persist indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that confidentiality obligations persist indefinitely, while the original agreement duration is limited to 18 months. This directly contradicts the specified duration of the agreement in the original text, creating an in-text contradiction as the duration of the entire agreement now conflicts with the duration of specific obligations within it.
